data.title: Corn barge bids flat, supported by export demand
data.description:
Spot basis bids for corn shipped by barge to U.S. Gulf Coast terminals were mostly steady on Monday, underpinned by export demand, traders said.

  • Though export demand has been solid, it could slow as competing supplies from Argentina and Brazil increasingly hit the global market, a trader said.

  • U.S. soybean export sales face intense competition from Brazilian supplies.

  • Traders will watch to see whether the U.S. Department of Agriculture adjusts its U.S. export estimates in a monthly supply/demand report due on Thursday.

  • USDA reported on Monday that 2 million metric tons of U.S. corn were inspected for export in the week that ended on April 2. That exceeded analysts' expectations of 1.3 million to 1.85 million metric tons.

  • Weekly U.S. soybean export inspections were 779,352 metric tons, compared with analysts' estimates of 400,000 to 750,000 metric tons.

  • CIF Gulf corn barges loaded in April were bid steady at 85 cents over Chicago Board of Trade May (CK26) futures.

  • FOB corn offers for vessels loaded in April remained at 98 cents over futures.

  • CIF Gulf soybean barges loaded in April were bid 2 cents higher at 75 cents over CBOT May (SK26) soybean futures.

  • FOB export premiums for April soybean vessels remained at about 92 cents over futures.

  • Traders continued to monitor the U.S. and Israeli war on Iran that has sent oil prices climbing. Iran pushed back against pressure to reopen the Strait of Hormuz while U.S. President Donald Trump warned the country could be "taken out" if it did not meet his Tuesday night deadline to reach a deal.

For displays of CIF basis, click on the codes in brackets:

U.S. CIF Gulf soybeans (GRYM)

U.S. CIF Gulf corn (GRYN)

data.title: Broadcom to supply custom TPUs and networking components to Google, expands Anthropic compute access
data.description:
Broadcom entered a long‑term agreement to develop and supply custom TPUs for Google and to supply networking and other components for Google AI racks through 2031.

Key Highlights:

  • Broadcom will develop and supply custom Tensor Processing Units for Google’s future TPU generations.
  • Broadcom signed a Supply Assurance Agreement to provide networking and other components for Google’s next‑gen AI racks through 2031.
  • Broadcom, Google and Anthropic expanded collaboration so Anthropic can access ~3.5 GW of next‑gen TPU‑based AI compute via Broadcom beginning in 2027.
  • Anthropic’s consumption of the expanded compute is contingent on its continued commercial success; parties are discussing operational and financial partners.

Original SEC Filing: Broadcom Inc. [ AVGO ] - 8-K - Apr. 06, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Clarification of RIWI's Private Placement Closing on March 10, 2026
data.description:
Toronto, Ontario--(Newsfile Corp. - April 6, 2026) - RIWI Corp. (the "Company" or "RIWI"), a market research platform, global trend-tracking and prediction technology firm, is issuing this news release to clarify disclosure in its news release dated February 27, 2026 and March 11, 2026, relating to its non-brokered private placement (the "Offering").

Clarification of Gross Proceeds and Debt Settlement

In connection with the Offering, the Company issued an aggregate of 8,322,220 units (the "Units") at a price of $0.30 per Unit, for an aggregate issuance value of $2,496,666.

The Company wishes to clarify that:

  • $2,024,970 represents gross cash proceeds raised from the Offering, and
  • $471,696 is deemed by the TSX Venture Exchange (the "Exchange") to represent shares issued in settlement of outstanding indebtedness (the "Debt Settlement").

Revised Offering Breakdown

In connection with the Offering, the Company issued an aggregate of 8,322,220 common shares, comprised of:

  • 6,749,900 Units issued for cash proceeds of $2,024,970, with each Unit consisting of one common share and one-half of one common share purchase warrant; and
  • 1,572,320 Units issued in connection with the Debt Settlement, for a deemed value of $471,696, with each Unit consisting of one common share and one-half of one common share purchase warrant.

Accordingly, the Company issued an aggregate of:

  • 8,322,220 common shares, and
  • 4,161,110 warrants.

Background to Debt Settlement

Following the announcement of the Offering and prior to its completion, the Company repaid certain outstanding debt obligations in the ordinary course of business using existing cash on hand. Certain creditors subsequently elected, of their own volition, to participate in the Offering. The repayment of such indebtedness was not conditional upon participation in the Offering. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Exchange has deemed these transactions to constitute a "shares for debt" arrangement under its policies, and accordingly the Company has presented the applicable portion of the Offering as a Debt Settlement.

Except as clarified herein, all other terms of the Offering remain as previously disclosed in the Company's press releases dated February 27, 2026 and March 11, 2026, including the warrant terms, insider participation and finder's fees, which were calculated based solely on the gross cash proceeds of the Offering.

The Offering remains subject to final acceptance of the Exchange. All securities issued are subject to a statutory hold period of four months and one day.

The net cash proceeds of $2,024,970 from the Offering are being used for general working capital and corporate purposes, including continued product development and commercialization activities. No proceeds were received in connection with the Debt Settlement.

Insiders of the Company participated in the Debt Settlement for a total of $471,696. Participation by the insiders constitutes a related party transaction as defined under Multilateral Instrument 61-101 ("MI 61-101"). The Company is relying on the exemptions from the formal valuation and minority shareholder approval requirements of MI 61-101 contained in sections 5.5(a) and 5.7(1)(a) of MI 61-101, as the fair market value of the participation in the Private Placement by insiders does not exceed 25% of the market capitalization of the Company, as determined in accordance with MI 61-101.

Engages Firm to Provide Capital Markets Advisory Services

RIWI has entered into an advisory agreement (the "Agreement") with JT Pacific Capital Partners Corp. ("JT Capital") to provide capital markets advisory and investor relations services.

The Agreement has a term of twelve (12) months commencing April 1, 2026, and may be terminated by either party in accordance with its terms. In consideration for the services to be provided, JT Capital will receive a monthly fee of $6,000 and will be granted 125,000 stock options (the "Options"). Each Option will be exercisable to acquire one common share of the Company at an exercise price equal to the Market Price (as defined by the Exchange) at the time of the grant. The Options will vest in equal quarterly instalments over a period of twelve (12) months from the date of grant, with the first vesting occurring three (3) months following the date of grant. The Options are granted pursuant to the Company's stock option plan and are subject to the approval of the TSX Venture Exchange.

JT Capital is a Vancouver-based capital markets advisory firm that provides strategic guidance to small and microcap growth companies. Its services include investor communications, capital markets advisory, and stakeholder engagement. JT Capital and its principals are arm's length to the Company. Certain principals of JT Capital hold securities of the Company.

This news release does not constitute an offer to sell or a solicitation of an offer to buy any securities, and there will be no sale of the securities in any jurisdiction in which such offer, solicitation or sale would be unlawful. The securities being offered have not been, nor will they be, registered under the United States Securities Act of 1933, as amended, and may not be offered or sold in the United States absent registration or an applicable exemption from registration requirements.

About RIWI

RIWI is a market research platform and global trend-tracking and prediction technology firm. On a monthly or annual subscription basis, RIWI offers its clients tracking surveys, continuous risk monitoring, predictive analytics and ad effectiveness tests in all countries. https://riwi.com

RIWI CORP.

Signed: "Greg Wong"

Greg Wong, Chief Executive Officer

For more information, please contact investors@riwi.com

Neither the TSX Venture Exchange nor its Regulation Services Provider (as that term is defined in the policies of the TSX Venture Exchange) accepts responsibility for the adequacy or accuracy of this release.

CAUTION REGARDING FORWARD-LOOKING INFORMATION:

Information and statements contained in this news release that are not historical facts are "forward-looking information" within the meaning of Canadian securities legislation that involves risks and uncertainties. Forward-looking information included herein is made as of the date of this news release and RIWI does not intend, and does not assume any obligation, to update forward-looking information unless required by applicable securities laws. Forward-looking information relates to future events or future performance and reflects management of the Company's expectations or beliefs regarding future events. This forward-looking information is based, in part, on assumptions and factors that may change or prove to be incorrect, thus causing actual results, performance or achievements to be materially different from those expressed or implied by forward-looking information.

THIS NEWS RELEASE IS INTENDED FOR DISTRIBUTION IN CANADA ONLY AND IS NOT INTENDED FOR DISTRIBUTION TO UNITED STATES NEWSWIRE SERVICES OR DISSEMINATION IN THE UNITED STATES.

data.title: Vizsla Silver: Nine Workers Taken from Mexico Mine Confirmed Dead, 1 Missing
data.description:
By Katherine Hamilton

Vizsla Silver said nine workers who were taken from its mining site in Mexico have been confirmed dead, while one remains missing.

In late January, the Canadian mining company said 10 people were taken from its silver-gold mining project in Mexico's Sinaloa.

Vizsla is in close contact with the family of the missing worker, and authorities are continuing to investigate, it said.

Write to Katherine Hamilton at katherine.hamilton@wsj.com

data.title: Trump threatens to take out Iran in 'one night' if deal is not done
data.description:
At a White House press conference outlining the rescue mission for the two US servicemen who ejected from an F-15 fighter jet, Trump said the US could take out the whole of Iran "in one night and that night could be tomorrow night".

Trump was referring to the deadline he has given Iran to reopen the strait of Hormuz, through which around 20% of the world’s oil supply travels. If Iran does not reopen the strait by 0100 BST on Tuesday, Trump has threatened major attacks against the country’s energy and transport infrastructure.

Commenting on how the US war with Iran is going, Trump said: "We're doing unbelievably well."

Asked about a potential ceasefire with Iran, Trump said the US has an "active willing participant on the other side that would like to be able to make a deal".

One reporter asked whether Trump was worried that his threat to burn power plants and bridges in Iran could amount to war crimes, but he said: "No, not at all. No. No, no. I hope I don’t have to do it."

In response to a question about whether he was winding down the war or escalating it, Trump doubled down on his threat to take out Iran’s energy infrastructure.

"I can’t tell you. I don’t know. It depends what they do," he said. "This is a critical period. They have a period of well, til tomorrow at eight o’clock. I gave them an extension. They asked for an extension of seven days, right…I gave them 11 actually. I thought it was inappropriate the day after Easter. I wanna be a nice person.

"They have til tomorrow and we’ll see what happens. I can tell you they’re negotiating we think in good faith. We’re gonna find out. We’re getting the help of some incredible countries that want this to be ended because it affects them also.

"A lot of people are affected by this but we’re giving them til tomorrow eight o’clock eastern time and after that they’re gonna have no bridges, they’re gonna have no power plants…Stone ages, yeah, stone ages."

Trump was asked whether he would make a deal that does not include the strait of Hormuz or whether that was now a top priority. He said: "I would say it’s a very big priority."

"To close the strait all you need is one terrorist that somehow has a truck loaded with cos you can carry on the trucks large trucks a water mine, drop them in the water and now you tell people that own ships that cost a billion dollars don’t worry about the mine," he said. "You can do that even just by saying we put mines in the water. So it’s not like the rest."

Trump went on to say that Iran was "at the weakest point" it’s ever been at.

"They have no navy. They have no air force. They have no anti aircraft, weaponry. They have no radar. They have no communication," he said.

"All I wanna see is I wanna have a safe world and you’re not gonna have a safe world. Israel will be gone. The Middle East will be gone and then they’re coming for Europe. And I have to tell you I’m very disappointed in Nato. Very. I think it’s a mark on Nato that will never disappear."

Asked if he was willing to end the conflict with Iran charging tolls for passengers through the strait, Trump said: "What about us charging tolls?

"I’d rather do that then let them have it, right? Why shouldn’t we? We’re the winner. We won, OK. They are militarily defeated.

"The only thing they have is the psychology of 'oh we’re gonna drop a couple of mines in the water', alright.

"We have a concept where we’ll charge tolls."

One reporter asked if in order for Iran to successfully meet the US deadline on Tuesday they have to make a deal, open the strait or both. Trump said: "We have to have a deal that’s acceptable to me. And part of that deal is going to be we want free traffic of oil and everything else.

Trump went on to threaten the decimation of every bridge in Iran.

"We have a plan - because of the power of our military - where every bridge in Iran will be decimated by 12 o’clock tomorrow night. Where every power plant in Iran will be out of business, burning, exploding and never to be used again. I mean complete demolition by 12 o’clock and it’ll happen over a period of four hours if we want it to," he said.

data.title: BNY, Robinhood Tapped for Important Roles in Trump Accounts Launch — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Andrew Welsch

Bank of New York Mellon and Robinhood Markets will serve important roles in helping get Trump Accounts — investment accounts for newborns and children — up and running, the Treasury Department announced Monday morning.

BNY will serve as a financial agent of the U.S. government, a designation that will permit it to manage the initial accounts and help develop a new Trump Accounts app, the agency said. BNY has teamed up with Robinhood, which will serve as a brokerage firm and initial trustee for Trump Accounts, according to the department's announcement.

Robin Vince, CEO of BNY, said in a statement that he sees the program as an opportunity to help "give more children a foundation for long-term financial security."

The Treasury Department also said that the National Design Studio, a government agency, and Robinhood are involved in developing the user interface for the Trump Accounts app. The Treasury Department said it would retain control over the app and operations for the initial accounts.

Representatives of BNY and Robinhood were unavailable for immediate comment on the terms of their agreements related to the program.

Congress authorized the Trump Accounts as part of the budget bill passed last year with the goal of helping parents save for their children's future. The accounts are structured as tax-deferred savings vehicles, akin to individual retirement accounts (IRA). Withdrawals can be taxed as ordinary income. They can also be subject to a 10% penalty if a withdrawal occurs before age 59 1/2 unless used for specific purposes, such as paying for college tuition. The annual contribution limit is $5,000.

The accounts are effectively an alternative to 529 plans, another tax-deferred savings vehicle for parents, albeit with different advantages. For example, withdrawals from a 529 plan for education expenses are tax-free. And unused funds in a 529 plan can be transferred to another child or they can be rolled over into a Roth IRA. Unlike a traditional IRA, distributions from a Roth IRA are tax-free.

Parents of a newborn have good reason to open a Trump Account because the government will automatically deposit $1,000 for babies born between Jan. 1, 2025 and Dec. 31, 2028. Employers can also contribute to these accounts, and some large corporations, such as Charles Schwab, have announced plans to match the government $1,000 contribution.

Some financial advisors have said parents of newborns should consider taking the free money, but may generally be better off relying on 529 plans for meeting their education savings goals.

Parents can also open accounts for older children provided they are under age 18.

Write to Andrew Welsch at andrew.welsch@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Geospace Technologies Cuts Workforce by 20%
data.description:
By Katherine Hamilton

Geospace Technologies has cut its global workforce by about 20%.

The manufacturer, which makes equipment for the oil and gas industry, said Monday the cuts were part of an evaluation of ways to operate more efficiently and profitably.

Geospace had 519 employees as of the end of September 2025, according to its latest annual filing.

Geospace expects the job cuts, along with other cost-cutting measures, are expected to produce about $10 million annual savings.

Its executive team implemented the organizational change, which resulted in the workforce reduction, at the end of the second quarter of 2026.

In connection with the job eliminations, Geospace anticipates $600,000 in termination costs in the fiscal second quarter and $700,000 in the fiscal third quarter. The charges are mostly related to employee transition, severance and benefits payments.

The Houston-based company has faced challenges, recording lower sales in several recent quarters. Leadership has cited macroeconomic volatility related to tariffs and inflation as the reason for lower-than-expected results.

Write to Katherine Hamilton at katherine.hamilton@wsj.com

data.title: Equities gain, oil settles higher as investors watch US-Iran standoff
data.description:
Key points:
  • Oil prices settle higher after choppy session
  • US, Iran weigh peace framework as Trump threatens Iran infrastructure attacks
  • Dollar dips, US Treasury yields barely lower

By Sinéad Carew and Gregor Stuart Hunter

U.S. equity indexes closed modestly higher and U.S. oil futures settled above $112 per barrel on Monday as investors waited for clarity on the prospects of a resolution to the war in the Middle East.

The U.S. and Iran exchanged verbal attacks while President Donald Trump reiterated threats to strike Iran unless Tehran makes a deal by Tuesday night.

Iran said on Monday it wanted a lasting end to the war with the U.S. and Israel, and pushed back against pressure to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Trump warned the country could be "taken out" if it did not meet his Tuesday night deadline.

Trump said at a press conference that the U.S. had an active and willing participant on the other side in talks with Iran. But he repeated threats to attack Iranian power plants and other key infrastructure, and U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said the largest volume of strikes since day one of the war would occur on Monday and warned of more on Tuesday. Meanwhile Iran's top joint military command described Trump's threats as "delusional."

This followed the U.S. president's expletive-laden Easter Sunday social media post, which threatened to target Iranian infrastructure unless it reopens the strait, through which a fifth of global energy traffic passes.

CRUDE RISES IN CHOPPY SESSION

Oil lost ground earlier in a choppy session as the U.S. and Iran weighed their next steps, but futures ultimately finished higher. U.S. crude NYMEX:CL1! settled up 0.78%, or 87 cents, at $112.41 a barrel while Brent ICEEUR:BRN1! settled at $109.77 per barrel, up 0.68%, or 74 cents.

On Wall Street, the S&P 500 and the Nasdaq registered their fourth advance in a row for the first time since late January, although gains were relatively small and trading volume was light after the long weekend.

The Dow Jones Industrial Average DJ:DJI rose 165.21 points, or 0.36%, to 46,669.88, the S&P 500 SP:SPX rose 29.14 points, or 0.44%, to 6,611.83 and the Nasdaq Composite TVC:IXIC rose 117.16 points, or 0.54%, to 21,996.34.

"The stock market has been stuck in neutral for most of the day. All the focus is on geopolitics, as traders are waiting to see if President Trump follows through on his threat to resume bombing Iranian energy infrastructure on Tuesday night," said Tim Ghriskey, senior portfolio strategist at Ingalls & Snyder in New York.

With some financial markets closed for the Easter Monday and Tomb-Sweeping Day holidays, MSCI's gauge of stocks across the globe EURONEXT:IACWI rose 3.47 points, or 0.35%, to 997.67.

TREASURY YIELDS HOLD STEADY

In currencies, the dollar index TVC:DXY, which measures the greenback against a basket of currencies including the yen and the euro, fell 0.21% to 99.99. But, with many Asian and European markets closed on Monday, liquidity was thin.

The euro FX:EURUSD was up 0.24% at $1.1543 while against the Japanese yen FX_IDC:USDJPY, the dollar strengthened 0.08% to 159.69.

On Friday, Japanese Finance Minister Satsuki Katayama put currency traders on notice, saying the government stands ready to act against speculative moves in foreign exchange markets as volatility has risen "significantly."

Yields on U.S. Treasuries were little changed with investors caught between optimism over reports of a ceasefire plan and unease over Trump's threat to escalate strikes on Iran.

"Markets are starting to digest that they can't take any single headline at face value. And part of that is that President Trump and Iran have been shifting their sentiment about how likely ceasefire negotiations are going to be," said Will Compernolle, macro strategist at FHN Financial in Chicago.

The yield on benchmark U.S. 10-year notes TVC:US10Y fell 0.5 basis points to 4.341% from 4.346%, while the 30-year bond (US30YT=RR) yield fell 1.1 basis points to 4.8948%.

The 2-year note (US2YT=RR) yield, which typically moves in step with interest rate expectations for the Federal Reserve, was flat at 3.852%.

Earlier in the day, data from the Institute for Supply Management showed that U.S. services sector growth slowed in March, while prices paid by businesses for inputs climbed to near a 3-1/2-year high, an early sign that the prolonged war with Iran was boosting inflation pressures.

In precious metals, gold prices edged lower as investors awaited further signals on the U.S.-Iran situation ahead of the Tuesday deadline.

Spot gold TVC:GOLD fell 0.42% to $4,656.21 an ounce while spot silver BIST:XAGUSD1! rose 0.01% to $73.00 an ounce.

www.reuters.com/world/iran

data.title: Murphy USA Appoints Smith CFO on Permanent Basis
data.description:
By Christopher Kuo

Murphy USA named Donald R. Smith, Jr., its chief financial officer on a permanent basis, effective immediately.

Smith was also appointed senior vice president for the gas and convenience retailer, the company said Monday. Since October, Smith has been interim CFO and vice president, chief accounting officer and treasurer, following the departure of Galagher Jeff.

Smith will retain the position of treasurer as CFO.

Smith has worked at Murphy USA since its 2013 spinoff from Murphy Oil. He previously worked for more than 14 years at KPMG.

His annual base salary will be $550,000, and he will receive a one-time cash bonus of $45,000, according to an SEC filing.

Write to chris.kuo@wsj.com

data.title: Cloudastructure to Participate in “Powering the AI Revolution” Virtual Conference Presented by Maxim Group LLC on Tuesday, April 7th at 9:00 a.m. EDT
data.description:
PALO ALTO, CA, April 06, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Cloudastructure , a leader in AI-powered video surveillance and remote guarding services, today announced that Chief Executive Officer James McCormick and Chief Financial Officer Greg Smitherman will be participating in the “Powering the AI Revolution:Building the Infrastructure Behind Generative AI” virtual conference, hosted by Maxim Group LLC, on Tuesday, April 7 at 9:00 a.m. EDT.

Cloudastructure will participate alongside other industry leaders in discussions focused on the rapidly expanding infrastructure needs driven by generative AI. As demand accelerates, companies are investing heavily in both physical and digital infrastructure to support this growth. During the conference, Maxim Group research analysts will engage with AI company executives who are at the forefront of delivering this capacity—spanning hyperscale data centers, power generation, cooling systems, and connectivity solutions.

Participants will explore the evolving AI infrastructure landscape, including key challenges such as land acquisition, capital deployment, construction sequencing, and the race to bring new capacity online to meet surging demand.

This conference will be live on Maxim Digital. To attend, sign up to become a Maxim Digital member.

Click here to learn more and reserve your seat.

ABOUT CLOUDASTRUCTURE

Headquartered in Palo Alto, California, Cloudastructure’s patented, advanced, award-winning security platform utilizes a scalable cloud-based architecture that features cloud video surveillance with proprietary, state-of-the-art AI/ML analytics, and a seamless remote guarding solution. The combination enables enterprise businesses to achieve proactive, end-to-end security, and pairs that platform with an attractive value proposition that eschews proprietary hardware and offers contract-free, month-to-month pricing and unlimited 24/7 support. With Cloudastructure, companies can achieve unparalleled situational awareness in real time and thereby stop crime as it is happening, while simultaneously achieving up to a 75% lower Total Cost of Ownership than other systems. For more information, visit https://www.cloudastructure.com/.

About Maxim Group LLC

Maxim Group LLC is a full-service investment banking, securities and wealth management firm headquartered in New York. The Firm provides a full array of financial services including investment banking; private wealth management; and global institutional equity, fixed-income and derivatives sales & trading, equity research and prime brokerage services. Maxim Group is a registered broker-dealer with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) and the Municipal Securities Rulemaking Board (MSRB) and is a member of FINRA SIPC, and NASDAQ. data.title: Vertical Aerospace Completes Piloted eVTOL Transition, Secures Up To $850M Financing
data.description:
Vertical Aerospace is beginning to show tangible progress at a time when sentiment has remained pressured, with the stock still down more than 50% year to date despite a modest premarket move higher. The company announced it has completed the first piloted thrustborne transition in a full-scale eVTOL aircraft under UK Civil Aviation Authority oversight, a step that could matter because it addresses one of the most technically demanding phases of flighttransferring lift from propellers to wings in real-world conditions at full scale. Management framed this as a meaningful validation point not just for its platform, but for the broader advanced air mobility narrative.

The flight, carried out on April 2 at Vertical Aerospace's Cotswold Airport test center and flown by pilot Paul Stone, represents the first half of a two-way transition sequence, moving from vertical takeoff into wingborne flight. The remaining legtransitioning back from wingborne flight to vertical landingstill needs to be completed, and could be a key unlock for achieving a fully runway-free operating profile for the company's Valo aircraft. That next step may carry outsized importance as the company continues progressing toward certification and real-world deployment.

At the same time, Vertical Aerospace pointed to an agreement in principle for a financing package of up to $850 million announced on March 30, which could provide both near-term liquidity and additional capital flexibility as it advances toward type certification and eventual commercial operations. CEO Stuart Simpson described the combination of technical progress, regulatory engagement, and capital access as a turning point, suggesting the company may now have the foundation to move toward service entry, though investors could still weigh execution risks, particularly with short interest at 6.2% of the float and commercialization still ahead.

data.title: Neuronetics to Cut Up to 5% of Staff as CFO Exits
data.description:
By Elias Schisgall

Neuronetics will cut up to 5% of its staff as part of a workforce reduction, and said its chief financial officer would step down.

The medical technology and healthcare company said Monday it expects to incur restructuring charges of about $200,000 related to the workforce cuts in the second quarter. It expects the restructuring to bring annualized cost savings between $2.5 million and $3 million, largely beginning in the third quarter.

The workforce reduction was launched on Thursday and is expected to be complete by mid-year, the company said.

Neuronetics also said that CFO Steven Pfanstiel would step down to pursue an outside opportunity.

He will remain in the role through May 1 to support a transition, and his decision to resign wasn't the result of any disagreement with Neuronetics, the company said.

It has launched a search to identify a new CFO.

The company also reaffirmed its financial guidance issued during a fourth-quarter earnings call in March.

The restructuring at Neuronetics comes as Pointillist Family Office Managing Member Jorey Chernett called on the company to explore strategic alternatives, including a sale of its transcranial magnetic stimulation business.

Pointillist is Neuronetics' largest shareholder with a roughly 14% stake, Chernett said.

Write to Elias Schisgall at elias.schisgall@wsj.com

data.title: PacifiCorp Lines Up $2.55 Billion LC and Matching Surety Facility for Oregon Judgments
data.description:
PacifiCorp arranged two complementary financing facilities to secure appeal-related obligations tied to Oregon wildfire judgments. The company signed a $2.55 billion, two-year standby letter of credit facility led by PNC Bank, available upon regulatory approvals, to collateralize surety bonds. In parallel, PacifiCorp executed a committed surety facility of up to $2.55 billion with Euler Hermes North America Insurance Company to issue supersedeas bonds, fully backed by qualifying letters of credit. The structures include customary covenants, cash collateralization triggers, premiums and fees tied to credit ratings, and an optional one-year extension on the surety facility.

Agreement 1: PacifiCorp Secures $2.55 Billion Two-Year Standby LC Facility Led by PNC Bank

  • Agreement type: Two-year multi-bank standby letter of credit facility
  • Counterparty: PNC Bank, as administrative agent, and other issuing banks; PNC Capital Markets as lead arranger
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 03 2026 / Sep 30 2026
  • Duration / Termination: 2 years from Issuance Effective Date
  • Reason: Secure collateral for appeal bonds on wildfire judgments

Agreement 2: PacifiCorp Establishes $2.55 Billion Surety Facility With Euler Hermes for Appeal Bonds

  • Agreement type: Committed surety facility for supersedeas bonds
  • Counterparty: Euler Hermes North America Insurance Company
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 02 2026 / Apr 02 2026
  • Duration / Termination: 2 years, plus 1-year extension option
  • Reason: Provide supersedeas bonds while Oregon judgments are on appeal

Original SEC Filing: PACIFICORP /OR/ [ PPWLO ] - 8-K - Apr. 06, 2026

data.title: Minerva Neurosciences sets June 3, 2026 annual meeting; stockholder notice deadlines announced
data.description:
Minerva Neurosciences announced its 2026 annual meeting is expected on June 3, 2026, and set related stockholder notice deadlines.

Key Highlights:

  • Annual meeting expected June 3, 2026, more than 30 days before the one-year anniversary of the 2025 meeting.
  • Stockholders wishing to bring business or nominate directors must deliver notice by close of business April 21, 2026.
  • Proposals for inclusion in the proxy under Rule 14a-8 must be received by close of business April 16, 2026.
  • All notices and proposals must comply with the Bylaws and Exchange Act rules and be sent to the Company’s Secretary at 1500 District Avenue, Burlington, MA 01803.

data.title: Exponent Names Pye as President and Anderson to Be Finance Chief
data.description:
By Katherine Hamilton

Exponent named a new president, chief financial officer and board chair.

The company on Monday named John Pye as president, effective May 1. Pye, 55, joined Exponent in 1999 and has been promoted most recently to vice president of global offices and innovation.

Exponent plans to pay Pye an annual base salary of $650,000, plus stock options.

Eric Anderson was named chief financial officer, succeeding Richard Schlenker on May 1. Schlenker has been nominated to stand for election to the board.

Anderson, 51, started at Exponent in 2003 and most recently was vice president of finance and accounting.

Exponent plans to pay Anderson an annual base salary of $525,000, plus stock options.

The engineering firm said Karen Richardson, a lead independent director on the board, will succeed Paul Johnston as board chair.

Johnston plans to retire from the board as of the annual stockholders meeting scheduled for June 4.

Write to Katherine Hamilton at katherine.hamilton@wsj.com

data.title: Grades ease from multiyear highs as WTI/Brent spread trades in a narrower range.
data.description:
Cash crude grades eased on Monday from multiyear highs touched last week as the spread between WTI and Brent traded in a more narrow range.

Mars eased to a premium of $8.75 to West Texas Intermediate crude at Cushing from a $15 premium on Thursday. It had climbed to a $17.50 premium, the highest since 2013, excluding in April 2020 when U.S. crude prices went negative and forced grades that trade as differential to it to trade at extremely high premiums.

WTI Midland priced at $4.25 premium on Monday, easing 45 cents from Thursday, when it touched its highest level since 2008, excluding in April 2020.

The Brent/WTI spread traded in a more narrow range of minus $10.70 and minus $12.15, compared with Thursday when it traded as high as minus $10.18 and as low as minus $12.39.

Iran on Monday rejected a ceasefire and said a permanent end to the war was necessary, and pushed back against pressure to reopen the Strait of Hormuz while U.S. President Donald Trump warned the country could be "taken out" if it did not meet his Tuesday night deadline to reach a deal.

Spot premiums for U.S. West Texas Intermediate crude have jumped to all-time highs as competition between Asian and European refiners for supply heats up to replace Middle Eastern oil flows disrupted by the Iran war, industry sources said.

Saudi Arabia has set the official selling price of May Arab Light crude oil to Asia at a record premium of $19.50 a barrel above the Oman/Dubai average, an increase of $17 from the previous month, Saudi Aramco said on Monday. The move could further push up demand for U.S. crude to Asia.

  • Light Louisiana Sweet for May delivery gained $3.25 to a midpoint of a $8.00 premium and was seen bid and offered between a $7.75 and $8.25 a barrel premium to U.S. crude futures ​

  • Mars Sour eased $6.25 to a midpoint of a $8.75 premium and was seen bid and offered between a $8.50 and $9 a barrel premium to U.S. crude futures ​

  • WTI Midland eased 45 cents to a midpoint of a $4.25 premium and was seen bid and offered between a $4.00 and $4.50 a barrel premium to U.S. crude futures ​

  • West Texas Sour firmed 50 cents to a midpoint of a $3.75 premium and was seen bid and offered between a $3.50 and $4.00 a barrel premium to U.S. crude futures ​

  • WTI at East Houston, also known as MEH, traded between a $6.90 and $7.30 a barrel premium to U.S. crude futures

  • ICE Brent June futures rose 74 cents to settle at $109.77 a barrel on Monday​.

  • WTI May crude futures rose 87 cents to settle at $112.41 a barrel on Monday​.

  • The Brent/WTI spread widened 25 cents to last trade at minus $11.24, after hitting a high of minus $10.70 and a low of minus $12.15.

data.title: Strait of Hormuz sees increased ship traffic ahead of Trump's deadline. Here's why oil prices are not budging.
data.description:
By Isabel Wang

There has been an increase in vessels transiting the Strait of Hormuz in recent days, but the number remains substantially lower than before the U.S. and Israel launched their attack on Iran in late February

The Strait of Hormuz has seen an uptick in vessel traffic recently.

The Strait of Hormuz, through which nearly one-fifth of the world's oil moves, saw an uptick in ship traffic over the weekend, with President Donald Trump's latest deadline for Iran to reopen the key waterway set for Tuesday evening.

Yet for all the optimism over tanker activity in the strait, the numbers tell a more cautious story: Transit volumes remain well below preconflict levels, and oil markets have barely reacted.

A total of 21 vessels transited through the Strait of Hormuz over the weekend, with 10 moving through on Saturday and 11 on Sunday - a "noticeable uptick" from the levels following the Feb. 28 launch of attacks on Iran by the U.S. and Israel, Dimitris Ampatzidis and Yuan Li, risk and compliance analysts at Kpler, said in a Monday client note.

That was the largest two-day transit volume since March 1, according to a MarketWatch calculation based on vessel-tracking data from MarineTraffic (see chart below).

SOURCE: KPLER RISK AND COMPLIANCE

The increase over the weekend reflects a modest increase in activity as more countries secure agreements with Iran to allow passage of their vessels through the chokepoint, although overall traffic remains "substantially far below" preconflict levels, said Edward Meir, president of the Commodity Research Group and an analyst at Marex.

"There is an uptick in traffic, but it's not really that significant," Meir said. He also attributed the tepid response in oil prices to an expectation of "a last-minute postponement, maybe not a deal, but a postponement allowing the two sides to continue talking."

In a Truth Social post on Sunday, Trump threatened to attack Iranian bridges and power plants unless Tehran reopens the Strait of Hormuz by 8 p.m. Eastern time on Tuesday. He repeated those threats Monday during a news briefing, saying the U.S. may consider taking a page from Tehran's playbook and charging a fee for ships to move through the strait.

Oil prices were modestly higher on Monday, with U.S. benchmark West Texas Intermediate crude for May delivery (CLK26) (CL.1) up 0.8% to settle at $112.41 barrel - the highest level since June 16, 2022.

June Brent crude (BRNM26) (BRN00) rose 0.7% to $109.77 a barrel, according to Dow Jones Market Data.

Meanwhile, Wall Street has been reading local dispatches from Citrini Research regarding the Strait of Hormuz in hopes of gaining more insight about activity in the crucial waterway. Citrini is the research outlet behind an artificial-intelligence doomsday report that went viral in February

There were no verified reports of physical attacks or incidents in the strait over the past two days, suggesting some near-term stability in the region. However, evolving political developments could still affect how vessels operate and the risks they face in the coming days, the Kpler analysts said.

Trump has already postponed his deadline for attacking Iran's energy infrastructure multiple times. On Sunday, Trump told Fox News that he believed he could reach a deal with Iran by Monday, then he turned back to threats, saying he would destroy all of Iran's power plants and bridges if the Strait of Hormuz wasn't reopened by Tuesday evening. Attacking civilian infrastructure could be considered a war crime under international law.

U.S. stocks closed higher on Monday on optimism around a potential cease-fire and an off-ramp to the war. The Dow Jones Industrial Average DJIA gained 0.4%, while the S&P 500 SPX also climbed 0.4% and the Nasdaq composite COMP advanced 0.5%, according to FactSet data.

-Isabel Wang

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: AMC, Cinemark Stocks Jump After Record Easter Weekend. Thank 'Super Mario.' — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Adam Clark and Janet H. Cho

AMC Entertainment and Cinemark Holdings shares were rising on Monday after the movie-theater operators notched record five-day Easter weekend showings. The Super Mario Galaxy Movie is driving attendance.

AMC shares were up 14.3% to $1.28 in early afternoon trading, while Cinemark was up 2.9% at $30.10. AMC said more than six million people attended its locations in the U.S. or internationally between April 1 and April 5.

"THE SUPER MARIO GALAXY MOVIE brought families, younger audiences, and longtime fans to our theatres in impressive numbers, in the highest grossing Easter holiday weekend ever for AMC and ODEON," AMC CEO Adam Aron said in a statement.

Cinemark CEO Sean Gamble said: "This record-breaking Easter weekend is a powerful reminder of the enduring consumer appeal for the shared, immersive experiences only movie theaters can provide," including merchandise sales tied to the Super Mario Galaxy movie. "Moviegoers turned out to experience the nostalgia and adventure of The Super Mario Galaxy Movie, while also enjoying a diverse lineup of films that offered something for everyone."

Universal Pictures — owned by Comcast — and Nintendo notched the biggest box office weekend of the year with The Super Mario Galaxy Movie, with an estimated $190.1 million in domestic ticket sales and $372.5 million in global ticket sales through Sunday.

The Super Mario movie kicked off the second quarter and follows the best post-pandemic first quarter for North American box office sales, according to Eric Handler, senior media and entertainment analyst for Roth. Hollywood's first-quarter box office revenue was $1.73 billion, up 21.8%, and surpassing Roth's $1.575 billion estimate.

"The [first] quarter had five movies surpass $100 million, led by Project Hail Mary ($177 million) and Avatar: Fire and Ash ($154 million), " he wrote in a research note. In comparison, the first quarter of 2025 had only two movies surpass $100 million. And first-quarter totals are still 28% below 2019.

Comcast stock was down 0.6%, while Nintendo rose 1.3% in Tokyo trading.

Write to Adam Clark at adam.clark@barrons.com and Janet H. Cho at janet.cho@dowjones.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.
